title: Savory Greek Egg Frittata
description: A salty, molten, brunch-mood Greek dish featuring red chilli powder and crispy feta. This shojin-friendly recipe serves a crowd and is perfect for a quick and easy breakfast or lunch.
instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil in a 10-inch oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat.
Add diced red onion and cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
Add minced garlic and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add crumbled feta cheese and cook until melted, about 2-3 minutes.
Add fresh thyme and cook for 1 minute, until wilted.
Crack in the eggs and season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Cook the eggs until the edges start to set, about 2-3 minutes.
Add red chilli powder and cook for an additional 30 seconds.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and cook until the eggs are set and puffed, about 8-10 minutes.
Remove from the oven and garnish with chopped parsley and feta-studded olives, if desired.
